message:
perf(storage): raise message insert batch size from 20 to 100

MESSAGE_INSERT_BATCH_SIZE caps how many freshly-deduped messages
get folded into one multi-row INSERT against frankensqlite. The
previous 20 was a deliberately conservative number from the early
batched-insert work, picked at a time when frankensqlite's
execute path was still maturing and shorter VALUE lists had a
material parse-time cost.

That cost has since been driven down (per the InsertConversation
TreePerfProfile sub-stage breakdown landed earlier today), and the
binding cost per batch is now dominated by the prepare/execute
round-trip rather than the literal length of the VALUES list.

Each row still binds 8 values, so 100 rows × 8 = 800 placeholders
— a comfortable distance below SQLite's default
`SQLITE_MAX_VARIABLE_NUMBER` of 999. (frankensqlite tracks the
SQLite default for this limit.)

Effect: fewer prepare/execute round-trips per indexer insert
sweep, especially on the hot path that replays a long thread of
new messages into an existing conversation row. Comment updated to
explain the new headroom rather than reference the obsolete
"shorter VALUE lists" rationale.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
